title: Preliminary Evaluation of Short- and Medium-duration Pigeonpea Genotypes in the Dry Season in Indonesia
abstract: Eighteen determinate and 14 indeterminate short-duration (SD) pigeon pea [Cajanus cajan] genotypes along with control cv. Mega (determinate), and 16 medium-duration (MD) genotypes along with a local cultivar as control were evaluated during the 1994 dry season at 4 locations in Indonesia (Muneng, Mojosari, Jambegede and Kendalpayak). Results showed that both SD and MD genotypes have a good yield potential for sowing in the dry season. All of the tested genotypes outyielded the controls, except indeterminate, SD type UPAS120.
